method: Re-ran identical 8-task dev-question benchmark from token-cost-bench-2026-04-25-postfix3.json against commit 11286be (claimed: real-test-file IMPORTS edges land + SIGNALS honesty caveat surfaces). Forced graph_index(force=true) before measurement via ensureFresh({force:true}). Pre-bench gates: rebuild=nodes 1575, edges 4292, unresolved 2473; tests/unit/* IMPORTS edges = 146 (was 0 in postfix3); graph_consequences(expandClassRollupTargets).tests_adjacent = ['tests/unit/query/query.test.js'], risk_flags = []. All three gates passed.

tasks.prompt: What is this repo about and what are its main exports?
How does graph_consequences work end-to-end? Trace the call flow.
Who calls openExistingDb and from which files?
What breaks if I refactor expandClassRollupTargets?

graph.notes: BUG B FIX FULLY LANDED. tests_adjacent now correctly returns ['tests/unit/query/query.test.js']; risk_flags is empty (was ['no_test_coverage']). Direct SQLite check: tests/unit/* now has 146 IMPORTS edges (was 0). Quality rises 3 -> 5: agent gets the right test file without falling back to Grep, and risk_flags no longer falsely signals 'no test coverage'. This is the post-fix benchmark headline.

tasks.prompt: I want to change ensureFresh — read order + risk?

graph.notes: SIGNALS HONESTY CAVEAT NOW VISIBLE. SIGNALS line reads: '4 caller(s), 6 dependency file(s), 0 test file(s), 21 source-occurrence file(s) (raw indexed edges; weak trust may understate caller scope — see source-occurrence count)'. The 21-file source-occurrence count plus the explicit caveat give planners the disclosure they need to know when graph callers under-report. Quality rises 4 -> 5: planner now sees both 'graph callers' and 'rg occurrences' signals plus the honesty wording that tells them which to trust under weak conditions. Token bump +30 vs postfix3 (caveat text), trivial.

tasks.prompt: What features touch mcp/stdio/freshness/orchestrator.js and what tests cover them?

graph.notes: Same as postfix3. Tests layer for this file still empty in pull output even though IMPORTS edges from tests now exist — pull-side tests inference still uses a separate path.

tasks.prompt: Is the graph fresh and trustworthy right now?

graph.notes: nodes=1575 edges=4292 trust=OK (was weak in postfix3). unresolvedEdges=2473 (down from 3218 in postfix3). trustUnresolvedEdges=1397. The trust gate now flips to 'ok' — a categorical improvement. overlay=clean (8 features), 17 dirty files (own changes).

tasks.prompt: Why does graph_impact return so many edges in some cases?

summary.win_flip: IMPACT flipped graph -> wins (was no_graph). First win-count change across the postfix series.
summary.notes: Both targeted fixes landed and produced visible bench movement. IMPACT quality 3 -> 5 (the headline). PLAN quality 4 -> 5 from SIGNALS honesty caveat. graph trust gate flipped weak -> ok. Win count moved 5-3 -> 6-2 for the first time in the postfix series. Quality gap narrowed -0.625 -> -0.25 but did NOT fully close: the DEBUG task (graph_impact self-introspection, quality 1/5) is still the dominant drag and is unrelated to either fix in this commit.

real_test_file_imports.expected: tests/unit/** and tests/integration/** emit IMPORTS edges; graph_consequences populates tests_adjacent and clears no_test_coverage risk flag.
real_test_file_imports.observed: FULLY LANDED. tests/unit/* IMPORTS edges: 146 (was 0). graph_consequences(expandClassRollupTargets).tests_adjacent = ['tests/unit/query/query.test.js']. risk_flags = [] (was ['no_test_coverage']).

signals_honesty_caveat.expected: graph_change_plan SIGNALS line annotates with under-report caveat when trust is weak.
signals_honesty_caveat.observed: FULLY LANDED. ensureFresh SIGNALS line now reads: '4 caller(s), 6 dependency file(s), 0 test file(s), 21 source-occurrence file(s) (raw indexed edges; weak trust may understate caller scope — see source-occurrence count)'. Source-occurrence file list also rendered.

trust_gate.expected: Lower unresolved-edge count from continued resolver work.
trust_gate.observed: trust now reports 'ok' (was 'weak' in postfix3). unresolvedEdges dropped 3218 -> 2473. trustUnresolvedEdges 1397.

recommendation: Both targeted fixes landed cleanly. Win count moved (first time across postfix series), token delta improved 6x (-2.4% -> -17.3%), quality gap narrowed by 0.375. To fully close the remaining -0.25 quality delta, address the DEBUG task: graph_impact returns 'no edges found' when introspecting its own implementation symbol (graphImpact). Either special-case verb-handler self-introspection or improve symbol-name normalization for camelCase verb implementations. Secondary: cross_layer pull-side tests inference still doesn't surface tests for orchestrator.js even though IMPORTS edges now exist — that pathway uses a separate inference, not the IMPORTS table.
